name: Refrigerator Day Beef Pot Pie
recipeIngredient: 3 medium red potatoes, boiled and cubed
2 cups leftover pot roast, bite-sized cubes
2 1/2 cups chicken broth or beef broth
1/4 tablespoon dried dill weed (about 1 tablespoon fresh)
recipeInstructions:
1. Boil red potatoes for 15 minutes, then cool and cut into cubes.
2. Use small can of sliced mushrooms.
3. Measure about 1 tablespoon dried dill.
4. Heat bacon in a skillet until bits have rendered; strain and reserve the bits.
5. In remaining fat, add onions and saute for 5 minutes or until tender.
6. Add garlic and mushrooms; continue sauteing for a couple of minutes or until cooked through.
7. Remove skillet from heat; strain contents through a sieve into a mixing bowl (add bacon bits to onions and mushrooms).
8. Stir in leftover pot roast and peas; set aside.
9. In a small saucepan, melt 3 tablespoons butter; add flour and cook for a few minutes to make a roux.
10. Add broth and whisk vigorously until thickened; bring to a simmer and cook for a few more minutes.
11. Stir in dill weed and pickle; adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.
12. Preheat oven to 475°F (245°C).
13. Pour sauce over beef, peas, etc.; season to taste with salt and pepper.
14. Transfer mixture to a 9-inch quarte
